title: A New Statue for Christopher Columbus Near the White House
summary: President Donald Trump has put a new statue of Christopher Columbus near the White House. This statue is a copy of one that was thrown into a harbor in Baltimore in 2020 during protests. Trump thinks Columbus is a hero because he led a voyage in 1492 that started European exploration in America. But some people are upset because Columbus hurt Native Americans when he arrived. The statue is made of marble and was made by a sculptor in Maryland. A group that owns the statue lent it to the government. Trump wants to honor Columbus, while others celebrate Indigenous Peoples Day instead. This shows different views about history.

What do some people celebrate instead of Columbus Day?

background_read: Christopher Columbus was an Italian explorer who sailed across the Atlantic Ocean in 1492 and landed in the Americas. This event is often seen as the start of European colonization, which led to new countries like the United States. However, Columbus and other Europeans also caused harm to Native American peoples through violence and disease. In recent years, some people have protested against statues of Columbus because of this history. President Trump supports honoring Columbus as a hero, while others want to recognize the contributions and suffering of Indigenous peoples on Indigenous Peoples Day. This debate shows how people remember history in different ways.
Article_Structure: WHO: President Donald Trump and a sculptor named Will Hemsley
WHAT: A replica statue of Christopher Columbus was placed near the White House
WHERE: Near the White House in Washington, D.C.
WHY: To honor Columbus as a hero, despite protests about his impact on Native Americans

perspectives.description: Columbus is a hero who should be honored for starting European exploration in America
Columbus hurt Native Americans, so statues of him should be removed
